attacking_style: Defensive racer who prioritizes position protection over offensive moves. Vulnerable in explosive final moments (500m) when marked by pure climbers or puncheurs.
team_tactics: Conserves domestiques strategically even when holding race lead, preferring personal marking of threats. Team tends to misread tactical situations (echelon positioning, final climb defense).
terrain_preference: Struggles in sudden accelerations and echelon-forming conditions. Exposed on explosive short finishes and when race fractures unexpectedly rather than controlled climbing.
vulnerability: Final 500m accelerations after controlling tempo; loses jerseys when forced to respond personally. Poor positioning in classics-style splits despite team pedigree.
key_patterns: Lost race lead via last-moment attacks despite controlling race beforehand (2022 Suisse)
Marks threats personally rather than using team, even when holding jersey (Tour de Suisse)
Poor echelon positioning/reading despite team's classics expertise requiring chase efforts (Giro 2020)
form_trajectory: negative: Lost yellow jersey in 2022 when unable to respond to explosive finale, suggesting diminished punch at decisive moments
